description:
On torch 2.13.0, Inductor fails to compile vLLM's w8a8 block-FP8 matmul kernels with a bare, message-less AssertionError originating from the FxGraphCache code path in codecache.py. The failure manifests as torch._dynamo.exc.BackendCompilerFailed with backend='inductor', affecting 150 tests in Kernels DeepGEMM Test (H100) covering test_w8a8_block_fp8_deep_gemm_matmul and test_w8a8_block_fp8_flashinfer_matmul, plus 17 tests in Kernels FusedMoE Layer Test (2 H100s) on fp8_blocked subtests. The internal Inductor stack trace is suppressed in CI because TORCHDYNAMO_VERBOSE defaults to 0, so only the bare assertion is captured.

This regression is confirmed new on torch 2.13 — the same vLLM commit passes all affected tests on 5 recent main Full CI builds running torch 2.12/nightly (builds 71969, 72021, 72067, 72234, 72341). The root cause was traced to PR #184193 and was fixed by reverting PR #187666 on the release/2.13 branch, which resolved all 75 block-fp8 compile failures. The revert unblocked the compile path but exposed a separate numerical mismatch issue tracked in #188152.

On torch 2.13.0, the Qwen2-VL multi-image generation test diverges from HuggingFace reference output on the CPU backend lane. The test test_multi_image_models[qwen2_vl-test_case40] fails an assertion checking that vLLM-generated tokens appear in HuggingFace's top-N logprobs. The two outputs agree for a long prefix describing a stop sign scene then diverge — a numerical drift in the Qwen2-VL multimodal path under torch 2.13 where Triton is disabled (CPU-only). The failure presents as an AssertionError in tests/models/utils.py with mismatched token sequences.

The regression is new on torch 2.13 and passes 5/5 on main builds running torch 2.11 (builds 73052, 72995, 72843, 72766, 72616). It reproduces with the same signature on two consecutive 2.13 builds (#72241 on June 15 and #72925 on June 18). The suspected cause is the oneDNN v3.12 update in PR #181222, though this was not conclusively confirmed. The fix was applied on the vLLM side by bumping the num_logprobs threshold in the test PR, treating it as borderline top-N drift rather than a correctness bug.

On torch 2.13.0, the prebuilt nixl_ep C++ extension fails to load with an undefined symbol error for c10::impl::cow::materialize_cow_storage(c10::StorageImpl&). The nixl_ep_cpp shared object was linked against an earlier libtorch that exported this c10 COW-storage symbol, but torch 2.13 no longer provides it. This breaks all 5 NixlConnector PD accuracy lanes including CrossLayer KV layout, DP EP, FlashInfer, Distributed, and Hybrid SSM jobs, all failing on test_nixl_and_nixl_ep_imports with an ImportError at extension load time.

The symbol removal was traced to PR #179063, which removed or refactored the materialize_cow_storage function from c10's copy-on-write storage implementation. The failure is confirmed new on torch 2.13 — all NixlConnector PD jobs pass on main (torch 2.11) across 5 recent Full CI builds (73052, 72995, 72843, 72766, 72616) and reproduces on two consecutive 2.13 builds (#72241 and #72925). The fix required rebuilding nixl against torch 2.13 headers, tracked in ai-dynamo/nixl#1798.

On torch 2.13.0, vLLM's standalone compile path crashes during engine initialization because FxGraphCache._save_graph unconditionally calls produce_guards_expression_with_source_info on the ShapeEnv, but vLLM's custom AlwaysHitShapeEnv subclass does not implement this method. The failure raises AttributeError: 'AlwaysHitShapeEnv' object has no attribute 'produce_guards_expression_with_source_info', originating from torch/_inductor/codecache.py line 2180, which crashes the EngineCore and causes the server to exit unexpectedly. Only test_standalone_compile_correctness in the PyTorch Compilation Unit Tests job is affected.

The produce_guards_expression_with_source_info method appears to be new or renamed in the torch 2.13 ShapeEnv API, and FxGraphCache._save_graph now unconditionally calls it, breaking any custom ShapeEnv subclass that does not provide it. The root cause was traced to PR #184193, and the fix was delivered in PR #187734. The failure is confirmed new on torch 2.13 and passes on main (torch 2.11), though the same-named job also fails on recent main builds with a different signature (NoneType has no attribute 'model' at backends.py:994), which is a separate vLLM-main regression unrelated to this issue.

On torch 2.13.0 with triton 3.7.1, heavy multimodal generation tests for chameleon, llava, and llava_next models fail with ValueError: Pointer argument cannot be accessed from Triton (cpu tensor?). The error occurs during Triton kernel execution in the RoPE (rotary position embedding) path for these multimodal models. Six tests fail in the Distributed Model Tests (2 GPUs) job: two chameleon broadcast test cases, two llava broadcast test cases, and two llava_next broadcast test cases, all in test_single_image_models_heavy.

The regression is confirmed new on the torch 2.13 / triton 3.7.1 stack — all 6 tests pass on main (torch 2.11) across 5 recent Full CI builds (73052, 72995, 72843, 72766, 72616) and reproduce on two consecutive 2.13 builds (#72241 and #72925). The root cause was traced to PR #179082, and fix PR #187733 with cherry-pick #187983 (commit af333cf2) was applied but proved insufficient — build #74324 (June 25) with the fix in the wheel still reproduced all 6 failures, so the issue was reopened for a follow-up fix.

On torch 2.13.0, the PyTorch Fullgraph test test_custom_compile_config[compilation_config5-facebook/opt-125m-model_kwargs5] fails with a pydantic ValidationError reporting that facebook/opt-125m is an unrecognized model without a model_type key in its config.json. The error occurs during ModelConfig initialization in vLLM's engine setup path, where the model registry fails to recognize opt-125m despite it having a valid model_type field in its HuggingFace config. The failure raises through vllm/engine/arg_utils.py during create_model_config.

The regression is new on torch 2.13 — it passes on main nightly build #73639 and main daily build #73541 (both running torch 2.11.0) but fails on the torch 2.13 test PR build #73764. The root cause is under investigation and may be a vLLM-side or transformers interaction rather than a direct torch regression, possibly related to a model config parsing change. It was filed under the torch 2.13 umbrella because it only reproduces on the 2.13 test branch with identical vLLM code.

On torch 2.13.0 with triton 3.7.1, the block-scaled fp8 MoE kernel tests fail with numerical mismatches after the Inductor compile crash from #187484 was fixed by reverting PR #187666. The kernels now compile and run but produce numerically wrong results, with torch.testing.assert_close reporting up to 50.9% mismatched elements and greatest absolute differences of 4.34 in some subtests. The failures span 38 tests across 5 jobs: Kernels FusedMoE Layer Test (2 H100s) with 13 failures, and Kernels MoE Tests 1, 3, 4, 5 with 6, 9, 6, and 4 failures respectively. All failing subtests are on the block-scaled fp8 path (fp8_blocked / block_shape1) while non-block per-tensor fp8 and bf16 subtests pass.

The huge relative differences (up to ~3.9e8) alongside moderate absolute differences suggest a block-scaling factor is being applied incorrectly in the block-fp8 dequantization or GEMM path under torch 2.13. The regression is confirmed new on torch 2.13 — the same jobs pass on main Full CI builds #73852, #73953, and #74168 with identical vLLM code and only the torch/triton wheel differing. This issue is in the same block-fp8 MoE area as #187484 but is a distinct failure mode: #187484 was a compile-time crash while this is a runtime numerical correctness regression that was only surfaced once the compile path was unblocked.
